---
title: "Purple Bluestem (Anatherum cretaceum)"
slug: "anatherum-cretaceum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622289723/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Purple Bluestem"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622289723/medium.jpg" alt="Purple Bluestem" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/plantmanryan">
plantmanryan</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341522457">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=131178)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its upright growth and purplish-tinged seed heads. In Florida, it is found in sandy, open habitats such as flatwoods and prairies. It grows actively during the warmer months and contributes to ground cover and habitat structure in native grassland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1619470-Anatherum-cretaceum)
-   [Local profile](https://fsus.ncbg.unc.edu/show-taxon-detail.php?taxonid=17427)
